channel.title: ainews — Karl's daily AI news
channel.description: Daily AI news, deduped + tagged for hotloop's workflows

item.title: NVIDIA and ServiceNow Partner on New Autonomous AI Agents for Enterprises
item.description: NVIDIA and ServiceNow are partnering to deliver autonomous AI agents into enterprise environments, combining NVIDIA's AI infrastructure with ServiceNow's workflow platform. The collaboration targets agentic AI deployment at scale, moving beyond simple prompts to multi-step task execution inside enterprise systems.

item.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
item.description: Google announced a partnership with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on a $3.5 million Future Vision film competition. The contest invites filmmakers to create works that imagine positive AI futures, with Google providing AI tooling for entrants.

item.title: How to Build In-Vehicle AI Agents with NVIDIA: From Cloud to Car
item.description: NVIDIA's developer blog provides a technical walkthrough of building multimodal agentic AI for automotive cockpits, covering the full cloud-to-car architecture. The guide addresses how in-vehicle agents can reason, plan, and respond across voice, vision, and sensor inputs using NVIDIA's DriveOS stack.

item.title: Building for the Rising Complexity of Agentic Systems with Extreme Co-Design
item.description: NVIDIA argues that as AI systems shift from single-model inference to multi-agent coordination, hardware, software, and AI model design must be co-designed together — not built in isolation. The post outlines an 'extreme co-design' framework for scaling agentic system complexity without compounding failure modes.

item.title: GPT-5.5 Instant System Card
item.description: OpenAI published the system card for GPT-5.5 Instant, documenting capabilities, evaluations, and safety testing for the new variant. The card details performance benchmarks and the model's positioning relative to other GPT-5 variants.

item.title: GPT-5.5 Instant: smarter, clearer, and more personalized
item.description: OpenAI has replaced ChatGPT's default model with GPT-5.5 Instant, which delivers 52.5% fewer hallucinations than GPT-5.3 Instant on high-stakes prompts across medicine, law, and finance. The model gains personalization by referencing past conversations, files, and Gmail — initially for Plus and Pro users — and ships to all ChatGPT tiers.

item.title: NZ firms warned overseas businesses aren't getting a return on AI - MPs report
item.description: A NZ Parliamentary report cautions local businesses that overseas AI investments often fail to generate returns, while still encouraging domestic adoption. The report references international evidence showing many enterprise AI deployments underperform on ROI.

item.title: Massey University study: generative AI is replacing entry-level creative roles in NZ
item.description: A Massey University pilot study finds generative AI is displacing junior roles in New Zealand creative industries, with productivity gains often captured by overseas firms rather than local agencies.

item.title: Five Eyes guidance: don't give agentic AI the keys to sensitive data
item.description: Five Eyes intelligence agencies (US, UK, Canada, Australia, NZ) issued joint guidance urging organisations to limit agentic AI access to sensitive data. The guidance is directly relevant to NZ insurers and government agencies considering autonomous workflows.

item.title: OpenAI and PwC collaborate to reimagine the office of the CFO
item.description: OpenAI and PwC have announced a partnership to deploy AI agents across enterprise finance functions, targeting forecasting, reporting, and internal controls. The collaboration positions AI agents as the primary interface for CFO-level decision workflows, building on PwC's existing enterprise client base.

item.title: Optimize Supply Chain Decision Systems Using NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills
item.description: NVIDIA's cuOpt library has introduced 'Agent Skills' — modular AI capabilities that let agents autonomously solve supply chain optimization problems including vehicle routing, scheduling, and capacity allocation. The post demonstrates integrating cuOpt skills into agent pipelines for real-time decision support under volatile demand.

item.title: BNZ books a $352m write-down as AI accelerates legacy software obsolescence
item.description: Bank of New Zealand recorded a $352 million write-down on legacy systems, citing AI-driven advancements that are rapidly aging existing software and lifting upgrade costs. The disclosure is the first material AI-related impairment in NZ banking and may foreshadow similar moves at peers.

item.title: Beca uses Microsoft Azure and GPT-5.1 to add AI to New Zealand Geospatial Data platform
item.description: Engineering firm Beca, working with Microsoft, integrated Azure OpenAI's GPT-5.1 into its BEYON system to add AI capabilities to the New Zealand Geospatial Data platform. The deployment focuses on natural-language querying of national infrastructure datasets.

item.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
item.description: Google's monthly AI updates recap for April 2026, covering Gemini 3.1 Ultra launch with 2M-token context window, Veo 3 video generation advances, and integrations across Google Workspace and Search.

item.title: Cisco acquires Astrix Security for ~$400M to monitor AI agent permissions
item.description: Cisco is acquiring Israeli startup Astrix Security, an Anthropic and Menlo Ventures-backed company that monitors and controls permissions granted to AI agents. The deal underscores how managing autonomous-agent identities is becoming a discrete enterprise security category.

item.title: Katie Haun closes $1B across two funds to back AI agent and financial-services startups
item.description: Former Andreessen Horowitz partner Katie Haun raised $1 billion across two funds to invest in early- and later-stage companies at the intersection of financial services, AI, and alternative assets. The expansion beyond crypto reflects renewed conviction in agentic AI applications.

item.title: Sierra raises $950M Series E at $15.8B valuation for AI customer-service agents
item.description: Bret Taylor's Sierra closed $950 million in Series E funding led by Tiger Global and GV at a $15.8 billion valuation, up from $10 billion in 2025. The company builds AI agents for customer service and reports rapid enterprise adoption.

item.title: Anthropic announces $1.5B joint venture with Blackstone, Goldman Sachs, and Hellman & Friedman to deploy Claude across PE-backed companies
item.description: Anthropic unveiled a $1.5 billion joint venture with Blackstone, Goldman Sachs, and Hellman & Friedman to industrialise Claude deployments across the firms' private-equity portfolios. The JV targets workflow rebuilds in customer support, finance, and coding rather than raw model access, signalling a shift toward managed enterprise AI deployment.

item.title: Compute Optimal Tokenization research sweeps tokenizers, params, and data across LLM compute budgets
item.description: Tomasz Limisiewicz announced "Compute Optimal Tokenization" research, sweeping tokenizers, model parameters, and training data across compute budgets to derive Chinchilla-style guidance for tokenizer choice. The work suggests tokenizer design is an underappreciated lever for compute efficiency at frontier scale.

item.title: OpenAI launches $10B "Deployment Company" JV with TPG and SoftBank to industrialise AI rollouts
item.description: OpenAI is forming a $10 billion joint venture with private-equity firms including TPG and SoftBank to deploy AI across roughly 2,000 portfolio companies. The vehicle mirrors Anthropic's smaller PE deal and emphasises selling end-to-end workflow transformation rather than API access.

item.title: DeepClaude — Claude Code agent loop integrated with DeepSeek V4 Pro
item.description: DeepClaude pairs Claude Code's agent loop with DeepSeek V4 Pro for code generation, attempting to combine Claude's tool-use orchestration with a cheaper Chinese open-weights backend. The project trended on Hacker News with 600+ points.

item.title: NIST CAISI evaluation: DeepSeek V4 Pro trails leading US AI models by about eight months
item.description: NIST's Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) assessed DeepSeek V4 Pro as the most capable Chinese model to date but found it lags top US systems by roughly eight months on capability benchmarks. The evaluation is the first formal US government performance gap measurement for a Chinese frontier model.

item.title: OpenAI employees raise alarm over ChatGPT's failure to escalate violence threats to law enforcement
item.description: Internal OpenAI staff, per WSJ reporting, flagged that ChatGPT failed to escalate or alert authorities when users described real-world violence plans, including a recent mass shooting case. The disclosures highlight gaps in OpenAI's safety review and incident-response protocols for users disclosing imminent harm.

item.title: Palo Alto Networks to acquire AI gateway startup Portkey for $120M-$140M
item.description: Palo Alto Networks is buying AI infrastructure startup Portkey for an estimated $120M-$140M, expanding its security stack into the AI gateway category that brokers, observes, and rate-limits LLM calls for enterprise agent deployments. Portkey was previously backed by Elevation Capital.

item.title: NZ Maritime Operations Centre upgrade adds AI for alarm consolidation and monitoring
item.description: Wellington's Maritime Operations Centre is rolling out an AI layer that consolidates the hundreds-to-thousands of alarms operators face into the two or three that actually need attention, per Kordia CEO Neil Livingston. He framed the system as assisting rather than replacing human operators in safety-critical maritime monitoring.

item.title: Chat with Qwen (with Deep Research and Web Dev)
item.description: Qwen launched a public chat interface with Deep Research and Web Dev modes, exposing its agentic research and code-generation capabilities directly to end users via chat.qwen.ai.

item.title: NCSC-NZ + Five Eyes issue joint guidance on careful adoption of agentic AI
item.description: New Zealand's National Cyber Security Centre, alongside Five Eyes partners, released joint guidance on agentic AI deployment. The document warns that LLM-powered agents inherit prompt-injection and data-poisoning risks and outlines security expectations across sectors.

item.title: Pentagon strikes AI deals with seven big tech firms after shunning Anthropic
item.description: The US Department of Defense awarded AI contracts to seven major tech vendors but excluded Anthropic, reportedly due to Anthropic's restrictions on military use. The deals widen access to frontier models for defence applications.

item.title: Anthropic opens Sydney office, names Theo Hourmouzis GM for Australia and New Zealand
item.description: Anthropic has formalised its Australia/New Zealand presence with a Sydney office and ex-Snowflake VP Theo Hourmouzis as General Manager. The move signals deeper enterprise sales motion in the region, alongside partnerships such as TrendAI's Claude Opus 4.7 deployment.

item.title: OpenAI to expand ChatGPT ads pilot to Australia, New Zealand and Canada
item.description: OpenAI is broadening its ChatGPT advertising pilot beyond the US into Australia, New Zealand and Canada in the coming weeks. Updated privacy terms now allow user data sharing with advertisers for targeted placements.
